# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create a simple GitHub Actions workflow for our application CI. On every push, install dependencies, run unit tests, build the application, and deploy to our staging environment. Focus on speed - we want fast feedback for developers. We'll add security scanning later.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI

on: [push]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
      
      - name: Set up Node.js environment
        uses: actions/setup-node@v1
        with:
          node-version: '14'
          
      - run: npm ci
        
      - run: npm test

  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    needs: test
    
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
      
      - name: Set up Node.js environment
        uses: actions/setup-node@v1
        with:
          node-version: '14'
          
      - run: npm ci
        
      - run: npm run build
  
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    needs: build
    
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
      
      - name: Set up Node.js environment
        uses: actions/setup-node@v1
        with:
          node-version: '14'
          
      - run: npm ci
        
      - name: Deploy to staging environment
        run: |
          # Code for deploying the application to your staging environment goes here. 
          # You may want to use tools like Ansible or a custom shell script for this step.
